When a user lands on a PLP, we know the position (index) of each product rendered. We need to capture this positional data in our analytics events to understand how product placement influences engagement and conversion within our Recommendations widget.

This will enable the D&A and Recommendations teams to analyze performance by position — e.g., do products in positions 1–5 convert better? Does a recommended product clicked from position 12 perform differently than one from position 3?

---

## 🔧 Technical Summary

For Promo Widget and [category pages](https://www.customink.com/products/hats/1), capture `index` and `item_id` for products across four events, with differing payload requirements:

**Full item list required (all products + their positions):**

* `recommendation_view`
* `view_promotion`

**Single item only (the interacted product):**

* `recommendation_click`
* `select_promotion`

**Example payload structure** (based on existing `view_item_list` pattern):

```
{
  "ecommerce": {
    "item_list_id": "42",
    "item_list_name": "all_products/drinkware/can coolers",
    "items": [
      { "index": 1, "item_id": 103600 },
      { "index": 2, "item_id": 125900 },
      ...
    ]
  },
  "event": "recommendation_view"
}
```

For click events, `items` will contain a single object:

```
"items": [{ "index": 12, "item_id": 459300 }]
```

---

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria / Rendering Conditions

* \[ \] Developers must add `data-testid` attributes to all new frontend components
* \[ \] `recommendation_view` fires with full `items` array — each product on the page includes `index` and `item_id`
* \[ \] `view_promotion` fires with full `items` array — each product includes `index` and `item_id`
* \[ \] `recommendation_click` fires with a single-item `items` array containing the clicked product's `index` and `item_id`
* \[ \] `select_promotion` fires with a single-item `items` array containing the selected product's `index` and `item_id`
* \[ \] `index` values are 1-based and reflect the rendered order of products on the page at time of event
* \[ \] Index order accounts for pagination or lazy-load (i.e., position 1 on page 2 ≠ index 1)
* \[ \] Events are validated in Amplitude and/or GA against expected payload structure
* \[ \] Edge case: if product list fails to load, no event fires (no empty or malformed payloads)

---

## 🚦 Release Timeline & Expectations

* **Target Release Date:** End of April 2026 (target)
* **Milestones / Deadlines:** TBD
* **Dependencies:**

    * Recommendations Service returning product list in rendered order
    * Alignment on event naming conventions with D&A team
    
* **Coordination Needs:**

    * D&A team to validate event schema and confirm tracking destination (Amplitude, GA, etc.)
    * If any additional clarification is needed on requirements or scope, reach out to the PM directly
    
* **Impact of Delay:** Recommendation and promotion events will lack positional context, limiting the team's ability to analyze placement effectiveness

---

## 🏁 Feature Flag

N/A

---

## 📊 Analytics & Tracking

* `recommendation_view` — full item list with index + item_id per product
* `recommendation_click` — single item with index + item_id of clicked product
* `view_promotion` — full item list with index + item_id per product
* `select_promotion` — single item with index + item_id of selected product

**Include metadata on all events:**

* `item_list_id`
* `item_list_name` (e.g., category/subcategory path)
* `index` (position on page, 1-based)
* `item_id`
* Page type (PLP, recommendation carousel, etc.)
* User state (logged in / guest)
* Session info

---

## 🧪 QA / Testing Requirements

* \[ \] Verify `recommendation_view` and `view_promotion` fire with complete item arrays on PLP load
* \[ \] Verify `recommendation_click` and `select_promotion` fire with only the clicked/selected product
* \[ \] Confirm `index` values are accurate and sequential based on rendered order
* \[ \] Test paginated PLPs — confirm index continues sequentially across pages (not reset to 1)
* \[ \] Test lazy-loaded PLPs — confirm index increments correctly as new products load
* \[ \] Validate no events fire if product list fails to render
* \[ \] Cross-platform: Desktop, Mobile (web)
* \[ \] Validate payloads in Amplitude / GA against agreed schema

---

## ⚠️ Risks & Considerations

* **Index accuracy on dynamic pages** — Lazy-load or infinite scroll could make index assignment tricky if not handled at render time
* **Pagination edge cases** — Index must not reset to 1 on subsequent pages; needs careful implementation
* **Event volume** — `recommendation_view` and `view_promotion` with full item arrays on large PLPs (40+ products) will be heavier payloads; confirm this is acceptable with D&A
* **Schema alignment** — Event names and payload structure must be agreed upon with D&A before dev starts to avoid rework

---

## 📎 Linked Designs / Specs / Tickets

* \[ \] D&A schema / tracking spec — TBD
* \[ \] Recommendations Service ticket — TBD

## 🎯 Business Context / Goal

When a user lands on a PLP, we know the position (index) of each product rendered. We need to capture this positional data in our analytics events to understand how product placement influences engagement and conversion within our Recommendations widget.

This will enable the D&A and Recommendations teams to analyze performance by position — e.g., do products in positions 1–5 convert better? Does a recommended product clicked from position 12 perform differently than one from position 3?

---

## 🔧 Technical Summary

For Promo Widget and [category pages](https://www.customink.com/products/hats/1), capture `index` and `item_id` for products across four events, with differing payload requirements:

**Full item list required (all products + their positions):**

* `recommendation_view`
* `view_promotion`

**Single item only (the interacted product):**

* `recommendation_click`
* `select_promotion`

**Example payload structure** (based on existing `view_item_list` pattern):

```
{
  "ecommerce": {
    "item_list_id": "42",
    "item_list_name": "all_products/drinkware/can coolers",
    "items": [
      { "index": 1, "item_id": 103600 },
      { "index": 2, "item_id": 125900 },
      ...
    ]
  },
  "event": "recommendation_view"
}
```

For click events, `items` will contain a single object:

```
"items": [{ "index": 12, "item_id": 459300 }]
```

---

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria / Rendering Conditions

* \[ \] Developers must add `data-testid` attributes to all new frontend components
* \[ \] `recommendation_view` fires with full `items` array — each product on the page includes `index` and `item_id`
* \[ \] `view_promotion` fires with full `items` array — each product includes `index` and `item_id`
* \[ \] `recommendation_click` fires with a single-item `items` array containing the clicked product's `index` and `item_id`
* \[ \] `select_promotion` fires with a single-item `items` array containing the selected product's `index` and `item_id`
* \[ \] `index` values are 1-based and reflect the rendered order of products on the page at time of event
* \[ \] Index order accounts for pagination or lazy-load (i.e., position 1 on page 2 ≠ index 1)
* \[ \] Events are validated in Amplitude and/or GA against expected payload structure
* \[ \] Edge case: if product list fails to load, no event fires (no empty or malformed payloads)

---

## 🚦 Release Timeline & Expectations

* **Target Release Date:** End of April 2026 (target)
* **Milestones / Deadlines:** TBD
* **Dependencies:**

    * Recommendations Service returning product list in rendered order
    * Alignment on event naming conventions with D&A team
    
* **Coordination Needs:**

    * D&A team to validate event schema and confirm tracking destination (Amplitude, GA, etc.)
    * If any additional clarification is needed on requirements or scope, reach out to the PM directly
    
* **Impact of Delay:** Recommendation and promotion events will lack positional context, limiting the team's ability to analyze placement effectiveness

---

## 🏁 Feature Flag

N/A

---

## 📊 Analytics & Tracking

* `recommendation_view` — full item list with index + item_id per product
* `recommendation_click` — single item with index + item_id of clicked product
* `view_promotion` — full item list with index + item_id per product
* `select_promotion` — single item with index + item_id of selected product

**Include metadata on all events:**

* `item_list_id`
* `item_list_name` (e.g., category/subcategory path)
* `index` (position on page, 1-based)
* `item_id`
* Page type (PLP, recommendation carousel, etc.)
* User state (logged in / guest)

content.text:
**RECO-738** — D&A: Product Position (Index) Tracking for Recommendation & Promotion Events

**Status:** To Do | **Assignee:** Andrii Popenko | **Target:** End of April 2026

**Goal:** Capture product position (`index`) in analytics events for recommendation/promotion widgets on PLPs to analyze placement effectiveness.

**Four events to update:**

| Event | Payload |
|---|---|
| `recommendation_view` | Full items array (all products + positions) |
| `view_promotion` | Full items array (all products + positions) |
| `recommendation_click` | Single item (clicked product + position) |
| `select_promotion` | Single item (selected product + position) |

Each item: `{ index: <1-based>, item_id: <id> }` plus metadata (`item_list_id`, `item_list_name`, page type, user state, session info).

**Key constraints:**
- Index is 1-based, reflects rendered order
- Index must NOT reset on pagination/lazy-load (continues sequentially)
- No events fire if product list fails to load
- `data-testid` attributes on all new frontend components

## Complete Analysis: Trending/Promo Product Analytics Events

I've thoroughly explored the codebase and found all files related to trending/promo product analytics events. Here's the complete breakdown:

---

### 1. **Main File: `trendingPromoProducts.ts`**
**Path:** `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/catalog/app/javascript/utils/metrics/trendingPromoProducts.ts`

Fires two events for trending products in TrendingCarousel:
- `firePromoTrendingRecommendationViewEvent`: Pushes `recommendation_view` event with `page_type` and `interaction_category`
- `firePromoTrendingRecommendationClickEvent`: Pushes `recommendation_click` event with `product_style_id`, `interaction_category`, `interaction_action`, and `interaction_label`

---

### 2. **TrendingCarousel Component**
**Path:** `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/catalog/app/javascript/features/TrendingProducts/TrendingCarousel.jsx`

Imports and uses the metrics from trendingPromoProducts.ts:
- Fires view event on IntersectionObserver when carousel is 10% visible (line 179)
- Fires click event when user clicks a product card (line 251)

---

### 3. **Files Firing `recommendation_view` and `recommendation_click` Events:**

#### a. `pdpMobileBudgetFriendlyReco.ts`
**Path:** `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/catalog/app/javascript/utils/metrics/pdpMobileBudgetFriendlyReco.ts`

- `fireBudgetFriendlyViewEvent()`: Fires `recommendation_view` with `page_type: 'pdp'` and `interaction_category: 'mobile_budget_friendly_reco'`
- `fireBudgetFriendlyClickEvent()`: Fires `recommendation_click` with `product_style_id`, `page_type`, `interaction_category`, and optional eventCallback

#### b. `favoritesSimilarProducts.ts`
**Path:** `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/catalog/app/javascript/utils/metrics/favoritesSimilarProducts.ts`

- `fireFavoritesSimilarRecommendationViewEvent()`: Fires `recommendation_view` with `page_type: 'favorites'` and `interaction_category: 'favorites_similar_products'`
- `fireFavoritesSimilarRecommendationClickEvent()`: Fires `recommendation_click` with similar structure
- `fireFavoritesTrendingRecommendationViewEvent()`: Fires `recommendation_view` with `interaction_category: 'favorites_trending_products'`
- `fireFavoritesTrendingRecommendationClickEvent()`: Corresponding click event

#### c. `pdpRelatedStyles.js`
**Path:** `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/catalog/app/javascript/utils/metrics/pdpRelatedStyles.js`

- `fireAlternativeProductInteractionGA4Event()`: Fires either `recommendation_view` or `recommendation_click` based on action parameter
- `fireGA4AlternativeDisplayEvent()`: Wrapper for module display
- `fireGA4AlternativeClickEventWithCallback()`: Fires `recommendation_click` with callback

#### d. `pdpRelatedStylesTabbedView.js`
**Path:** `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/catalog/app/javascript/utils/metrics/pdpRelatedStylesTabbedView.js`

- `fireViewedTabbedViewBreakoutEvent()`: Fires `recommendation_view` with tab-specific interaction categories
- `fireClickTabbedViewBreakoutEvent()`: Fires `recommendation_click` with dynamic interaction_category based on active tab
- Various arrow/tab interaction functions

---

### 4. **Files Firing `view_promotion` and `select_promotion` Events:**

#### a. `listing.ts`
**Path:** `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/catalog/app/javascript/src/shared/gtm/listing.ts`

**Key Pattern with `index` and `item_id`:**
- `selectItemEcomEvent()` (lines 76-107): Uses `view_item_list` event
  - Enriches item with `index: cardIndex` (line 91)
  - Gets `item_list_name` from getListingImpressionName() (line 92)
  - Uses ecommerce structure with `item_list_id`, `item_list_name`, and items array
  
- `selectCategoryEcomEvent()` (lines 109-123): Fires `select_promotion` for category cards
- `viewCategoryEcomEvent()` (lines 125-134): Uses IntersectionObserver to fire `view_promotion` when category card is 60% visible (line 161)

#### b. `marchPromoBanner.ts`
**Path:** `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/catalog/app/javascript/utils/metrics/marchPromoBanner.ts`

- `fireMarchPromoRenderedGA4Event()`: Fires `view_promotion` with ecommerce base data
- `fireMarchPromoInteractionGA4Event()`: Fires `select_promotion` with promotion item structure (item_id, item_name, item_brand, item_category, item_category3, item_product_type)

---

### 5. **`view_item_list` Event Implementation Pattern:**

**Path:** `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/catalog/app/javascript/algolia_listing_pages/useProductAnalyticsEvents.js`

Key pattern with `index` and `item_id`:
```javascript
const items = hits.map((hit, index) => 
  getItemEcomerceData(hit, index + 1)  // index is 1-based position
);

fireEcommerceEvent(
  getEcommerceEventPayload(items, categoryId, categoryTree),
  'view_item_list',
  {search_term: getQueryParam('keyword')}
);
```

From `productsAnalytics.js`:
- `getItemEcomerceData()` (lines 74-79): Returns `{index, item_id: Number(hit.product_id)}`
- `getEcommerceEventPayload()` (lines 81-87): Returns `{item_list_id, item_list_name, items}`

---

### 6. **Shared Analytics/Metrics Utilities:**

#### a. `gtm/base.ts`
**Path:** `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/catalog/app/javascript/src/shared/gtm/base.ts`

Core dataLayer push functions:
- `gtmEvent()`: Pushes `ci.catalog.*` events
- `gtmEcomEvent()`: Pushes ecommerce events with proper clearing (`ecommerce: null` first)

#### b. `gtm/types.ts`
**Path:** `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/catalog/app/javascript/src/shared/gtm/types.ts`

Defines all types:
- `EcommerceEventName`: Includes `'view_item_list'`, `'select_item'`, `'view_promotion'`, `'select_promotion'`
- `EcommerceItem`: Interface with `index`, `item_id`, `item_name`, `item_list_name`, etc.
- `PromotionData` and `PromotionItem` interfaces for promotion events

#### c. `productsAnalytics.js`
**Path:** `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/catalog/app/javascript/algolia_listing_pages/utils/productsAnalytics.js`

Utility functions:
- `fireEcommerceEvent()`: Main function that clears ecommerce layer first, then pushes event with ecommerce payload
- `getEcommerceEventPayload()`: Creates structure with `item_list_id`, `item_list_name`, `items`

#### d. `analytics.js`
**Path:** `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/catalog/app/javascript/utils/analytics.js`

Common dataLayer push patterns:
- `pushToDataLayer()`: Direct window.dataLayer.push wrapper
- `pushInteractionToDatalayer()`: Helper for interaction events

---

### Summary of Event Flow:

**Pattern 1: recommendation_* events (simple)**
```javascript
window.dataLayer?.push({
  event: 'recommendation_view' | 'recommendation_click',
  page_type?: string,
  interaction_category: string,
  interaction_action?: string,
  interaction_label?: string,
  product_style_id?: string | number
});
```

**Pattern 2: view_item_list / select_item (ecommerce)**
```javascript
window.dataLayer.push({ecommerce: null});  // Clear first
window.dataLayer.push({
  event: 'view_item_list' | 'select_item',
  ecommerce: {
    item_list_id: number,
    item_list_name: string,
    items: [{
      index: number,        // 1-based position
      item_id: number | string,
      item_name?: string,
      ...
    }]
  },
  search_term?: string
});
```

**Pattern 3: view_promotion / select_promotion (ecommerce)**
```javascript
window.dataLayer.push({ecommerce: null});  // Clear first
window.dataLayer.push({
  event: 'view_promotion' | 'select_promotion',
  ecommerce: {
    promotion_id?: string,
    promotion_name?: string,
    creative_name?: string,
    creative_slot?: string,
    items?: [{
      item_id: string,
      item_name: string,
      ...
    }]
  }
});
```

# Code Quality Skill

Run comprehensive code quality checks and automatically fix issues in TypeScript/NestJS projects.

## Workflow

Execute quality checks and fixes in this exact order:

### 1. Auto-fix linting

```bash
npm run lint:fix
```

Automatically fixes ESLint violations where possible.

### 2. Format code

```bash
npm run format
```

Applies Prettier formatting to all files.

### 3. Check types

```bash
npm run type-check
```

Identifies TypeScript compilation errors.

### 4. Verify all checks pass

```bash
npm run code-quality-check
```

Runs full validation: lint + format + type-check. Must all pass.

### 5. Fix remaining issues with AI

If errors remain after steps 1-4:
1. Analyze error output from step 4
2. Manually fix issues that auto-fixers couldn't resolve
3. Re-run step 4 to verify
4. Repeat until all checks pass

## Common Issues

### ESLint Errors

**No console logs**
- Replace `console.log()` with `Logger` from `@nestjs/common`

**No relative imports**
- Use path aliases: `@modules/`, `@utils/`, `@dto/`, etc.

**Explicit return types required**
- Add return types to all functions: `(): Promise<void>`

**Import order**
- Groups: builtin → external → internal → type
- Newline required after imports

### TypeScript Errors

**Type safety**
- Enable strict mode checks
- Use `@ts-expect-error` with explanation only when unavoidable

**Missing properties**
- Ensure all required interface properties are implemented

## After Running Checks

If issues remain after auto-fixes:
1. Review error output carefully
2. Fix remaining issues manually
3. Re-run verification
4. Ensure all checks pass before committing

### `TrendingBanner.jsx`
- View event (`fireGARenderedEvent`): builds 1-based `items` array from `products` and passes it
- Click event (`fireGAClickedEvent`): passes `(product, productIndex + 1)` — the 1-based index

```markdown
### Stakeholder Overview _[(learn more)](https://app.getguru.com/card/TGyLkrnc/Pull-Review-Stakeholder-Overview)_

Adds product position (`index`) and `item_id` tracking to recommendation and promotion analytics events fired by the Trending Products widgets (TrendingCarousel and TrendingBanner). This enables the D&A and Recommendations teams to analyze how product placement within these widgets influences engagement and conversion — e.g., do products in positions 1–5 convert better than those in position 12?

### Risk Estimate _[(learn more)](https://app.getguru.com/card/iMnRRRjT/Pull-Request-Risk-Estimate)_

- ✅ Negligible risk!

Analytics-only change. No UI changes, no feature flag required. Existing event names are preserved — payloads are enriched with additional `ecommerce` data. Worst case on failure: events fire without position data (current behavior).

### Changes

**`utils/metrics/trendingPromoProducts.ts`**
- `firePromoTrendingRecommendationViewEvent` — now accepts `items` array (`{index, item_id}[]`) and `categoryId`; pushes `ecommerce` payload with `item_list_id`, `item_list_name`, and full items list
- `firePromoTrendingRecommendationClickEvent` — now accepts `{product, index, categoryId, categoryName}`; pushes `ecommerce` payload with single-item array
- Both events now clear the ecommerce layer first (`{ecommerce: null}`) per GA4 best practices

**`utils/metrics/marchPromoBanner.ts`**
- `fireMarchPromoRenderedGA4Event` — now accepts `items: EcommerceItem[]` and includes them in `view_promotion` ecommerce payload
- `fireMarchPromoInteractionGA4Event` — now accepts `index` param; adds `index` to the promotion item in `select_promotion`
- Both events now clear the ecommerce layer first

**`features/TrendingProducts/TrendingCarousel.jsx`**
- Builds 1-based `items` array from `products` and passes to view event with `categoryId`/`categoryName`
- Passes `{product, index, categoryId, categoryName}` to click event

**`features/TrendingProducts/TrendingBanner.jsx`**
- Builds 1-based `mappedItems` array and passes to `fireGARenderedEvent`
- Passes `(product, productIndex + 1)` to `fireGAClickedEvent`

##### Updated Dependencies
- None

### Project Link

[RECO-738](https://customink.atlassian.net/browse/RECO-738) — D&A: Product Position (Index) Tracking for Recommendation & Promotion Events

### Screenshots

N/A — analytics-only change, no visual modifications.

### Notes

_Recommended reading: [Code Review guide](https://github.com/customink/guides/blob/master/operations/code-review/README.md)_

- Index values are **1-based** per the ticket requirements
- Payload structure follows the existing `view_item_list` pattern in `productsAnalytics.js`
- No existing tests for these files; no tests were broken

### Optional Tasks

- [ ] Include 🎥 Instructions
- [ ] Update the readme (README.md)
- [ ] Update the API or architecture docs (e.g. docs/api.md)

##### Library-Specific
- [ ] Increment the changelog (CHANGELOG.md)
- [ ] Increment the version number (lib/version.rb)
- [ ] [Release & Tag][release] the version above in Github

[release]: https://docs.github.com/en/github/administering-a-repository/managing-releases-in-a-repository

##### Performance
- No new queries introduced.

##### Data & Analytics Dependencies
- **GA tagging changes**: Yes — four events enriched with `ecommerce.items` containing `index` and `item_id`. D&A team should validate event schema in Amplitude/GA.
- No schema changes for Data Engineering.
```

message.content: Unmemoized mappedItems causes infinite useEffect re-runsMedium SeveritymappedItems is computed via products.map(...) inline on every render, producing a new array reference each time. Including it in the useEffect dependency array at line 201 causes the effect to re-run on every render, tearing down and recreating the IntersectionObserver unnecessarily. The hasFiredViewEvent ref guard prevents duplicate event firing, but the constant observer churn is wasteful and could theoretically prevent the observer callback from ever firing under rapid re-renders. Wrapping mappedItems in useMemo keyed on products would restore the previously-stable dependency behavior.
